title: Residence permit humanitarian non-temporary
content:
Residence permit humanitarian non-temporary

Last update: 19 December 2025

You want to apply for a residence permit humanitarian non-temporary. Find out what the requirements are and how to apply for the residence permit on non-temporary grounds.

Previous residence permit

Requirements

Process and costs

Application forms

The residence permit

See also

Previous residence permit

You can apply for a residence permit humanitarian non-temporary, if you had a residence permit for 1 of these residence purposes:

Residence with a family member or relative

Residence as a minor child with a child protection order

Residence as a victim or witness of human trafficking

Residence as a victim of domestic or honour-related violence

Residence because you cannot leave the Netherlands through no fault of your own

Residence for medical treatment

Requirements

Show details Hide details

Requirements after residence with a family member

Requirements that apply to each family member

There was no reason to withdraw your previous residence permit.

You have fulfilled the civic integration requirement. This means you have passed the civic integration exam. This does not apply after residence with your parent as a minor child. Sometimes you do not have to fulfill the integration requirement. Find out more about the civic integration requirement on our web page Civic integration for more secure residence permit and naturalisation.

Requirements per family member

You have had a residence permit for 5 years for one of these residence purposes:

Residence with your spouse or partner

Residence with your parent as an adult child

Residence with your child as their parent

Residence with another family member

You have had a residence permit for 1 year for residence as a minor child, with your parent, adoptive parent or foster parent.

You are a child born in the Netherlands, you have a residence permit for stay with parent, and your parent has a residence permit on non-temporary grounds.

Your family member is a Dutch national or has a residence permit for a non-temporary purpose at the day of the application.

After residence with a family member with a European Blue Card

You have had a residence permit continuously for 5 years for residence with your family member with a European Blue Card. This means the following is the case:

during the past 2 years, you had a residence permit to stay with this family member in the Netherlands;

before that, for at least 3 continuous years, you had a residence permit to live with this family member in another EU country.

Special individual circumstances after residence as a family member or relative

You have or had a residence permit for residence as a family member or relative.

You do not meet the other requirements that apply to the residence permit humanitarian non-temporary.

Because of special and individual circumstances, you cannot return to your country of origin. This is the country of your nationality. Special and individual circumstances are, for example:

The situation of (single) women in your country of origin.

There is no good shelter for you in your country of origin.

You have children who were born in or go to school in the Netherlands.

You are a victim of violence within the family.

The ties you have with the Netherlands.

Requirements after death of family member

You have or had a residence permit for residence with your spouse or partner. Or you have or had a residence permit for residence with your parent as a child.

Your family member was a Dutch national or had a residence permit for a non-temporary purpose.

You have not moved your main residence outside the Netherlands.

Requirements after residence as a child with a child protection order

You meet these 3 requirements:

You had a residence permit humanitarian temporary for at least 1 year. You received the residence permit under the policy that applies since 1 October 2019. The reason was that the court placed you under supervision.

You are still under supervision.

Your country of origin is still unable to take over the child protection order. This was made clear in the advice of the Repatriation and Departure Service (in Dutch: Dienst Terugkeer en Vertrek or DTenV).

Or you meet this requirement:

You have had a residence permit humanitarian temporary for 3 years because you were under a child protection order.

Do you get the residence permit? Then the parents, minor brothers and sisters residing in the Netherlands can also apply for a residence permit.

Requirements after residence as a victim of domestic or honour-related violence

You have had a residence permit for 1 year as a victim of domestic or honour-related violence.

The threat of domestic or honour-related violence still exists. Does the threat no longer exist? Sometimes you can get a residence permit humanitarian non-temporary because of your personal situation. The IND calls this urgent humanitarian reasons. The IND looks at these circumstances as a minimum:

The situation of (single) women in your country of origin.

There is no good shelter for you as a victim in your country of origin.

You have children who were born in or go to school in the Netherlands.

The use of violence within the family.

The ties you have with the Netherlands.

Requirements after residence as a victim or witness reporting human trafficking

You have a residence permit because you are a victim of human trafficking.

You have reported human trafficking. Or you have cooperated in the criminal investigation in a different way. One of these situations apply:

The Public Prosecutor will proceed to prosecute the criminal offence. This means that the suspect will appear before court.

The Public Prosecutor decided not to prosecute a criminal offense for human trafficking. You have filed a complaint with the court against this decision of the Public Prosecutor. Your complaint has been declared justified and the Public Prosecutor must now initiate criminal proceedings for human trafficking because of that. Please enclose a copy of the court decision, in which your complaint has been justified, with your application.

The criminal investigation is still in progress. You have had the residence permit as a victim of human trafficking for at least 3 years.

You have had a residence permit for 1 year as a victim of human trafficking who cannot or does not want to report this to the police. You cannot cooperate in the criminal investigation for one of these reasons:

The same threat still exists.

You still have a physical or mental illness.

You are still a minor (under the age of 18).

Assessment of personal situation

Do you not meet one of these requirements? Or did you have a residence permit because you were a witness of human trafficking and reported this to the police? Because of your personal situation, you may be able to get a residence permit humanitarian non-temporary. The IND looks at reasons that have to do with human trafficking. For example one of these reasons:

The risk of vengeance on you or your family. The IND checks whether the government in your country of origin is able or willing to protect you.

The risk that you will be charged with an offence in your country of origin. For example with prostitution.

The possibilities that you have in your country of origin to be included in society again.

Requirements after residence because it is not your fault that you cannot leave the Netherlands

You have had a residence permit humanitarian temporary for 3 years because you are not able to leave the Netherlands.

You still meet the requirements for the residence permit humanitarian temporary.

There are no other reasons to refuse the residence permit.

Conditions after residence for medical treatment

You have had a residence permit for medical treatment for at least 3 years. Or your departure has been postponed for 1 year under Section 64 of the Aliens Act (in Dutch: Vreemdelingenwet or VW). And immediately afterwards you had a residence permit for medical treatment for 2 years.

Your medical treatment is necessary for at least 1 more year.

During those 3 years there was no reason to withdraw your residence permit.

You still meet the requirements for the residence permit medical treatment.

Other rules may apply for Turkish citizens and their family members.

Process and costs

These are the steps in the application process for the residence permit

Show details Hide details

1. Check requirements

A valid provisional residence permit (MVV) is needed for this application

Make sure you meet the requirements.

1. Check requirements

Make sure you meet the requirements.

2. Collect documents

The written application form lists which documents you need. Collect all documents before you apply. You can upload the documents in the online application.

Have official foreign documents legalised and translated into Dutch, English, French or German.

2. Collect documents

The written application form lists which documents you need. Collect all documents before you apply. You can upload the documents in the online application.

Have official foreign documents legalised and translated into Dutch, English, French or German.

3. Apply

You can submit this application online or in writing by post.

4. Pay for application

The application costs € 254,00. For all fees and exceptions, visit our web page Fees: costs of an application.

If you apply online, you pay the costs (fees) immediately with IDEAL. Will you submit the application in writing? You will receive a letter that explains how you can pay.

3. Wait for decision on application

The IND must take a decision within 90 days. This is called the decision period.

We can extend the decision period if it takes longer to decide. For example, because the application is not complete. In that case, the IND will send you a letter.

Follow the application in My IND.

5. Wait for decision on application

The IND must take a decision within 90 days. This is called the decision period.

We can extend the decision period if it takes longer to decide. For example, because the application is not complete. In that case, the IND will send you a letter.

Follow the application in My IND.

4. Decision on application

You will receive a message with the decision. The decision will be positive or negative.

Positive decision: you will get an MVV and a residence permit.

Negative decision: you will not receive an MVV and residence permit. You can object against this decision.

6. Decision on application

You will receive the decision in a letter. The decision will be positive or negative.

Positive decision: you will get a residence permit.

Negative decision: you will not receive a residence permit. You can object against this decision.

7. Collect residence permit

When the residence permit is ready at an IND desk, you will receive a letter. You must make an appointment to collect it. Make an appointment to collect the residence document.

Application forms

In these situations you can submit this application online. • You have stayed in the Netherlands for more than 5 years as a spouse or partner. • As a minor child in the Netherlands, you have stayed with your parents for more than 1 year. • You were born in the Netherlands and have stayed with your parents. • Your sponsor (spouse, partner or parent) has passed away. • You don’t have a residence permit based on Article 8 ECHR. To apply online you need DigiD with SMS verification and internet banking (iDEAL).

Online application form Continued residence after a stay as a family member

Apply online You need a DigiD

Written application forms

If you are unable to submit this application online, download the form and send it by post.

Application for the purpose of residence of 'non temporary humanitarian' (foreign national) 7527 (PDF, 1.09 MB)

The residence permit

What else you need to know about the residence permit:

TB test

Did you indicate at the time of the application that you will take a TB (tuberculosis) test? Then make an appointment at the Public Health Service (in Dutch: Gemeenschappelijke Gezondheidsdienst or GGD). Do this within 3 months after you have received the residence permit. Take with you to the appointment:

Appendix TB test referral form 7604(PDF, 219.94 KB)

Working with the residence permit

It says on the back of your residence permit whether you are allowed to work. With the residence permit humanitarian non-temporary, you are free to work in the Netherlands. It says on your residence permit ‘free to work. Work permit (TWV) not required’ (in Dutch: arbeid vrij toegestaan. TWV niet vereist). The shortening TWV stands for tewerkstellingsvergunning.

Legal obligations

You have an obligation to provide information. This means that you must notify the IND of changes in your situation. Find out more about your obligation to provide information.

The IND checks whether you meet your obligations. If you do not, you can get an administrative fine.

Validity period of the residence permit

The residence permit humanitarian non-temporary is valid for 5 years.

Type of residence permit

The residence permit is type I, temporary regular residence permit.

Other applications

Replace residence permit

Has your residence permit been damaged, lost or stolen? Then have the residence permit replaced. Visit the web page residence permit damaged or change of personal details or residence permit lost or stolen.

Extend residence permit

Will your residence permit expire soon? Then you can extend the residence permit.

Permanent residence permit

Have you had a residence permit for 5 years? Then you can apply for a permanent residence permit.

See also

Living in the Netherlands

Travelling with a residence permit and a return visa

Previous residence permit

Requirements

Process and costs

Application forms

The residence permit

See also

You can apply for a residence permit humanitarian non-temporary, if you had a residence permit for 1 of these residence purposes:

Residence as a minor child with a child protection order
Residence as a victim or witness of human trafficking
Residence as a victim of domestic or honour-related violence
Residence because you cannot leave the Netherlands through no fault of your own
Residence for medical treatment
Requirements
Show details Hide details

Requirements that apply to each family member

You have had a residence permit continuously for 5 years for residence with your family member with a European Blue Card. This means the following is the case:
during the past 2 years, you had a residence permit to stay with this family member in the Netherlands;
before that, for at least 3 continuous years, you had a residence permit to live with this family member in another EU country.
Special individual circumstances after residence as a family member or relative
You have or had a residence permit for residence as a family member or relative.
You do not meet the other requirements that apply to the residence permit humanitarian non-temporary.
Because of special and individual circumstances, you cannot return to your country of origin. This is the country of your nationality. Special and individual circumstances are, for example:
The situation of (single) women in your country of origin.
There is no good shelter for you in your country of origin.
You have children who were born in or go to school in the Netherlands.
You are a victim of violence within the family.
The ties you have with the Netherlands.
Requirements after death of family member
You have or had a residence permit for residence with your spouse or partner. Or you have or had a residence permit for residence with your parent as a child.
Your family member was a Dutch national or had a residence permit for a non-temporary purpose.
You have not moved your main residence outside the Netherlands.
Requirements after residence as a child with a child protection order
You meet these 3 requirements:

You had a residence permit humanitarian temporary for at least 1 year. You received the residence permit under the policy that applies since 1 October 2019. The reason was that the court placed you under supervision.
You are still under supervision.

Your country of origin is still unable to take over the child protection order. This was made clear in the advice of the Repatriation and Departure Service (in Dutch: Dienst Terugkeer en Vertrek or DTenV).
Or you meet this requirement:
You have had a residence permit humanitarian temporary for 3 years because you were under a child protection order.
Do you get the residence permit? Then the parents, minor brothers and sisters residing in the Netherlands can also apply for a residence permit.
Requirements after residence as a victim of domestic or honour-related violence
You have had a residence permit for 1 year as a victim of domestic or honour-related violence.

The threat of domestic or honour-related violence still exists. Does the threat no longer exist? Sometimes you can get a residence permit humanitarian non-temporary because of your personal situation. The IND calls this urgent humanitarian reasons. The IND looks at these circumstances as a minimum:
The situation of (single) women in your country of origin.
There is no good shelter for you as a victim in your country of origin.
You have children who were born in or go to school in the Netherlands.
The use of violence within the family.
The ties you have with the Netherlands.
Requirements after residence as a victim or witness reporting human trafficking
You have a residence permit because you are a victim of human trafficking.
You have reported human trafficking. Or you have cooperated in the criminal investigation in a different way. One of these situations apply:
The Public Prosecutor will proceed to prosecute the criminal offence. This means that the suspect will appear before court.

The Public Prosecutor decided not to prosecute a criminal offense for human trafficking. You have filed a complaint with the court against this decision of the Public Prosecutor. Your complaint has been declared justified and the Public Prosecutor must now initiate criminal proceedings for human trafficking because of that. Please enclose a copy of the court decision, in which your complaint has been justified, with your application.
The criminal investigation is still in progress. You have had the residence permit as a victim of human trafficking for at least 3 years.

You have had a residence permit for 1 year as a victim of human trafficking who cannot or does not want to report this to the police. You cannot cooperate in the criminal investigation for one of these reasons:
The same threat still exists.
You still have a physical or mental illness.
You are still a minor (under the age of 18).

Do you not meet one of these requirements? Or did you have a residence permit because you were a witness of human trafficking and reported this to the police? Because of your personal situation, you may be able to get a residence permit humanitarian non-temporary. The IND looks at reasons that have to do with human trafficking. For example one of these reasons:
The risk of vengeance on you or your family. The IND checks whether the government in your country of origin is able or willing to protect you.
The risk that you will be charged with an offence in your country of origin. For example with prostitution.
The possibilities that you have in your country of origin to be included in society again.
Requirements after residence because it is not your fault that you cannot leave the Netherlands
You have had a residence permit humanitarian temporary for 3 years because you are not able to leave the Netherlands.
You still meet the requirements for the residence permit humanitarian temporary.
There are no other reasons to refuse the residence permit.
Conditions after residence for medical treatment

You have had a residence permit for medical treatment for at least 3 years. Or your departure has been postponed for 1 year under Section 64 of the Aliens Act (in Dutch: Vreemdelingenwet or VW). And immediately afterwards you had a residence permit for medical treatment for 2 years.
Your medical treatment is necessary for at least 1 more year.
During those 3 years there was no reason to withdraw your residence permit.
You still meet the requirements for the residence permit medical treatment.
Other rules may apply for Turkish citizens and their family members.

title: Staying in the Netherlands if you no longer live with an EU citizen
content:
Staying in the Netherlands if you no longer live with an EU citizen

Last update: 8 July 2025

You had the right of residence in the Netherlands under EU law because you lived with a family member or partner from the EU, EEA or Switzerland. You no longer live together. For example because your family member or partner died or your relationship has ended. Find out if you are allowed to continue to live in the Netherlands.

Situations in which you may continue to live in the Netherlands

Apply for a new residence document

Inform the IND when no longer living together

See also

Situations in which you may continue to live in the Netherlands

Do you no longer live with your family member or partner from the EU, EEA or Switzerland? Then you can stay in the Netherlands in some situations. From now on, we will call your family member or partner a citizen of the Union.

You have EU, EEA or Swiss nationality

Then you can continue to live in the Netherlands. However, there are requirements. Find out what the requirements are on the page Living in the Netherlands as an EU, EEA or Swiss citizen.

You have another nationality

You have another nationality and no longer live with the citizen of the Union. Select your situation below and find out whether you can continue to stay in the Netherlands. Is the citizen of the Union a Dutch national? Then the situations only apply to you if the Dutch national has made use of EU law. Find out when EU law applies to Dutch nationals.

Show details Hide details

You have been in the Netherlands for 5 years or longer

You may have permanent residency. Find out more on the page Permanent residence permit for Union citizens and their family.

Union citizen has died

You had been living with the citizen of the Union in the Netherlands for at least 1 year at the time of death. Then you can continue to live in the Netherlands.

You are the child of the citizen of the Union who has left or died

You are studying in the Netherlands. You and the parent caring for you are allowed to stay in the Netherlands until you complete your study programme.

Marriage or relationship with the citizen of the Union has ended

Has your marriage or relationship with the citizen of the Union ended? Then you are allowed to continue to live in the Netherlands in these situations:

Your marriage or relationship lasted at least 3 years at the start of the divorce or ending of the relationship. And you lived at least 1 year of the marriage or relationship in the Netherlands.

You have been awarded custody of the (minor) children. Or you have parental access rights and the judge has ordered that you must be able to see your children in the Netherlands.

There is a special or difficult situation. For example, you were the victim of domestic violence during your marriage or relationship. Or you are the victim of honour-based violence.

Your income is high enough (income requirement)

You must have enough income to be allowed to continue to live in the Netherlands. We call this the income requirement. For the income requirement, it does not matter which nationality you have. In these situations, your income is high enough:

You are an employee or entrepreneur. You carry out genuine and effective work. This means that you work at least 40% of the regular full working week. Or you earn at least 50% of the social assistance benefit applicable to you.

You have an income that is the same or higher than the standard amount applicable to your family situation. And you have health insurance in the Netherlands.

Apply for a new residence document

Do you no longer live with the citizen of the Union and are you allowed to continue to live in the Netherlands? Then you sometimes need a new residence document. Look at the following situations and check whether you must apply for a new residence document:

Your residence document is still valid. You will keep this document and do not need to apply for a new one. However, you must notify the IND that the situation has changed.

Your residence document is no longer valid. And you do not have permanent residency yet. You must apply for a new residence document. Find out more on the page Verification against EU law for Union citizen family members.

You have been living in the Netherlands continuously for 5 years. You may have permanent residency. You must apply for a new residence document. Find out more on the page Permanent residence permit for Union citizens and their family.

Inform the IND when no longer living together

Do you no longer live together with the citizen of the Union? Then inform us. You can use this using this form:

Reporting form for foreign nationals 7597 (PDF, 216.2 KB)

See also

Permanent residence permit for Union citizens and their family

Verification against EU law for Union citizen family members

Rules and regulations

Article 8.14 Aliens Decree (Vreemdelingenbesluit 2000)

Article 8.15 Aliens Decree (Vreemdelingenbesluit 2000)​

Articles 12 and 13 Directive 2004/38/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council

Situations in which you may continue to live in the Netherlands

Apply for a new residence document

Inform the IND when no longer living together

See also

Your Europe

title: Permanent residency
content:
Permanent residency

title: Permanent residence permit
content:
Permanent residence permit

Last update: 10 June 2025

You want to apply for a permanent residence permit. Find out what the requirements are and how you can apply for permanent residency.

Do you have an asylum residence permit? Visit the web page Permanent asylum residency. Are you an (ex-)privileged? Visit the web page Permanent residence permit for (ex-)privileged persons and their family members.

Requirements

Process and costs

Application forms

The residence permit

See also

Do you have an asylum residence permit? Visit the web page Permanent asylum residency. Are you an (ex-)privileged? Visit the web page Permanent residence permit for (ex-)privileged persons and their family members.

Requirements

These are the requirements for a permanent residence permit.

You meet the general requirements that apply to everyone.

You have had a valid Dutch residence permit for at least 5 consecutive years. There are exceptions to the 5-year period for permanent residency.

Only years from the age of 8 on count towards the 5-year period. Children can only apply for a permanent residence permit, if they are at least 13 years old and have had a valid residence permit from the age of 8.

You had your main residence in the Netherlands during the entire 5-year period.

You have always extended your residence permit in time.

You have always met the requirements for your residence permit. And you still meet those requirements.

You now have a valid residence permit for a non-temporary residence purpose.

During the application for the residence permit you have now, you did not give any false information. And you did not withhold information.

You meet the income requirements.

You are registered in the Personal Records Database (in Dutch: Basisregistratie Personen or BRP) at your local town hall. The BRP is the population register of the Netherlands.

You have passed the civic integration exam at at least level A2. Or you have been exempted from the civic integration exam. Read more about civic integration and a permanent residence permit.

Were you born in the Netherlands and are you now 18 years old? Or were you under 3 years old when you came to live in the Netherlands and have you always lived in the Netherlands since then? In that case, the IND can reject your application only if you are a danger to the public order or national security.

Residence permit as a long-term EU resident

Are you applying for a permanent residence permit? Then the IND will first check if you meet the requirements for the residence permit for a long-term EU resident. You do not have to do anything for this yourself.

With the residence permit for a long-term EU resident, it will be easier for you to apply for a residence permit in another EU country. You must, however, meet the requirements for this residence permit in the other EU country.

See the list of EU countries with a residence permit for long-term EU residents.

Process and costs

These are the steps for applying for the residence permit.

Show details Hide details

1. Check requirements

A valid provisional residence permit (MVV) is needed for this application

Make sure you meet the requirements.

1. Check requirements

Make sure you meet the requirements.

2. Collect documents

The written application form lists which documents you need. Collect all documents before you apply. You can upload the documents in the online application.

Have official foreign documents legalised and translated into Dutch, English, French or German.

2. Collect documents

The written application form lists which documents you need. Collect all documents before you apply. You can upload the documents in the online application.

Have official foreign documents legalised and translated into Dutch, English, French or German.

3. Apply

You can submit the application online or in writing by post.

Your residence permit must still be valid when you apply. You can apply from 3 months before your current residence permit expires.

Have you already lived in the Netherlands with a residence permit for more than 5 years? Then you can choose when to apply yourself. You do not have to wait until your residence permit has almost expired.

4. Pay for the application

The application costs € 254,00. Are you under 18? A different amount applies. For all fees and exceptions, visit our web page Fees: application costs.

When you apply online, you pay the fee immediately with iDEAL internet banking. Are you submitting a written application? You will receive a letter which explains how you can pay.

5. Wait for decision on application

You may await the processing of your application in the Netherlands. The IND must take a decision within 6 months. This is called the decision period.

We can extend the decision period if it takes longer to decide. For example, because the application is not complete. In that case, the IND will send a message.

Follow the application in My IND.

IND late with decision

Has the decision period passed and you still have not received a decision on your application? Find out what you can do if the IND is late deciding.

Working during the application

The back of your current residence document states whether you are allowed to work and whether you need a work permit (TWV). This is the employment status. Did you apply before the end date of your current permit? Then you will keep the employment status on your current residence document while your application is being processed. Even if your permit expires in the meantime and you have not yet received a new residence document.

6. Decision on application

The IND first checks whether you meet the requirements of the residence permit for long-term EU residents. If you do not meet those requirements or the requirements for a permanent residence permit either, we will see if we can extend your current residence permit.

The decision is in the form of a letter and is positive or negative. The decision is the result of a reassessment of your situation.

Positive decision: you will receive a residence permit for long-term EU residents, a permanent residence permit or we will extend your current residence permit.

Negative decision: you will not receive a residence permit. You can object against this decision.

7. Collect residence permit

When the residence permit is ready at an IND desk, you will receive a letter. You must make an appointment to collect it. Make an appointment to collect the residence document.

Application forms

You can submit this application online. You must have DigiD with SMS verification and internet banking (iDEAL).

Online application form application regular permanent residence or EU-long term resident

Apply online You need a DigiD

Written application forms

Download the form if you prefer to fill in the application in writing and send it by post.

This form is only available in Dutch:

Aanvraag verblijfsvergunning EU langdurig ingezetene of een verblijfsvergunning regulier voor onbepaalde tijd 6009 (PDF, 810.19 KB)

The residence permit

Find out what else you need to know about the residence permit.

Working with the residence permit

With a permanent residence permit you are free to work in the Netherlands. Your employer does not need a work permit (in Dutch: tewerkstellingsvergunning or TWV). On the back of the residence permit it says ‘Free to work. Work permit not required (in Dutch: Arbeid vrij toegestaan. TWV niet vereist). TWV stands for tewerkstellingsvergunning which means work permit in Dutch.

Legal obligations

You have an obligation to provide information. This means that you must inform the IND of changes in your situation. Read more about your obligation to provide information on the page Obligations foreign national and sponsor of family member.

The IND checks whether you meet your obligations. If you do not, you may receive an administrative fine.

Validity of the residence permit

A permanent residence permit has no end date. The residence document is valid for 5 years and you can renew it every 5 years.

Type of residence permit

The permanent regular residence permit is type II.

The residence permit for long-term EU residents is type V.

Other applications

Replacement of the residence permit

Has the residence permit been damaged, lost or stolen? Then have the residence permit replaced. Visit the web page Residence permit damaged or change details or Residence permit lost or stolen.

Renew residence permit

If it has almost expired you can renew the residence permit.

See also

Living in the Netherlands

Travelling with a residence permit and a return visa

Apply for a residence permit for long-term EU residents

Permanent residence permit for privileged and former privileged persons and family members

Permanent asylum residency

Requirements

Process and costs

Application forms

The residence permit

See also

title: Permanent residence permit for (ex-)privileged persons and their family members
content:
Permanent residence permit for (ex-)privileged persons and their family members

Last update: 25 February 2026

Are you a (ex-)privileged person in the Netherlands? Or are you a family member of a (ex-)privileged person in the Netherlands? And do you want to live permanently in the Netherlands? You need a permanent residence permit. Find out what the requirements are and how you can apply for the residence permit.

What is a (ex-)privileged person?

Requirements

Process and costs

Application form

The residence permit

See also

What is a (ex-)privileged person?

A privileged person is someone who has a special privileged status as a diplomat, consular employee or international organisation. An ex-privileged person is someone who no longer has the privileged status, through actions other than their own.

Requirements

Show details Hide details

(ex-)Privileged person

These requirements apply to you as an (ex-)privileged person.

One of these situations apply to you.

You are or were employed at a foreign diplomatic or consular post. You were a member of the administrative, technical or support staff.

You are or were employed at an international organisation.

You are or were a senior management member, including the head, of an international organisation.

Directly before the application you have lived in the Netherlands for 10 years or more with a privileged status. Did you work for an international organisation? In that case you can add periods in which you have had a Dutch residence permit to the 10 years.

You meet the income requirements.

You do not pose a threat to public order and national security.

You are registered in the Personal Records Database (BRP) of your city or town (in Dutch: gemeente).

You passed the civic integration exam or you do not have to take the civic integration exam. Find out more on the page Civic integration for more secure residence permit and naturalisation.

International organisation exists for less than 10 years

Is the international organisation where you work allowed to exist no longer than 10 years? And have you lived in the Netherlands for 5 continuous years as an employee of this organisation? Then you can apply for a permanent residence permit.

Family members of (ex-) privileged persons

Are you a family member of a (ex-)privileged person? Then you can also apply for a permanent residence permit. For you the following requirements apply:

You are 18 or older at the time of the application for the residence permit. If you are under 18, you need to apply for a temporary residence permit.

You have lived in the Netherlands for at least 10 years with a privileged status. Did your family member with the privileged status work for an international organisation? In that case you can add periods in which you have had a Dutch residence permit to the 10 years.

You do not pose a threat to public order and national security.

You are registered in the Personal Records Database (BRP) of your city or town (in Dutch: gemeente).

You passed the civic integration exam or you do not have to take the civic integration exam. Find out more on the page Civic integration for more secure residence permit and naturalisation.

International organisation exists for less than 10 years

Does the international organisation for which your family member has worked shorter than 10 years? Then you can apply for permanent residence if you have lived in the Netherlands for 5 continuous years while your family member worked as an employee of this international organisation.

Process and costs

These are the steps in the application process for the residence permit.

Show details Hide details

1. Check requirements

A valid provisional residence permit (MVV) is needed for this application

Make sure you meet the requirements.

1. Check requirements

Make sure you meet the requirements.

2. Collect documents

The application form lists which documents you need. Collect all documents before you apply.

Have official foreign documents legalised and translated into Dutch, English, French or German.

2. Collect documents

The application form lists which documents you need. Collect all documents before you apply.

Have official foreign documents legalised and translated into Dutch, English, French or German.

3. Apply

You submit the application in writing by post.

4. Pay for the application

The application costs € 254,00. Are you under 18? Then a different amount applies. For all fees and exceptions, visit our web page Fees: application costs.

You will receive a letter which explains how you can pay.

5. Wait for decision on application

You may await the processing of your application in the Netherlands. The IND must take a decision within 6 months. This is called the decision period.

We can extend the decision period if it takes longer to decide. For example, because the application is not complete. In that case, the IND will send a message.

Follow the application in My IND.

IND late with decision

Has the decision period passed and you still have not received a decision on your application? Find out what you can do if the IND is late deciding.

Working during the application

The back of your current residence document states whether you are allowed to work and whether you need a work permit (TWV). This is the employment status. Did you apply before the end date of your current permit? Then you will keep the employment status on your current residence document while your application is being processed. Even if your permit expires in the meantime and you have not yet received a new residence document.

3. Decision on application

You will receive a message with the decision. The decision will be positive or negative.

Positive decision: you will get an MVV and a residence permit.

Negative decision: you will not receive an MVV and residence permit. You can object against this decision.

6. Decision on application

You will receive the decision in a letter. The decision will be positive or negative.

Positive decision: you will get a residence permit.

Negative decision: you will not receive a residence permit. You can object against this decision.

7. Collect residence permit

When the residence permit is ready at an IND desk, you will receive a letter. You must make an appointment to collect it. Make an appointment to collect the residence document.

Application form

Written application forms

Download the form, fill it in and send it by post.

Aanvraag voor een EU verblijfsvergunning voor langdurig ingezetene of een verblijfsvergunning regulier voor onbepaalde tijd - 6009 (Only available in Dutch)

Aanvraag verblijfsvergunning EU langdurig ingezetene of een verblijfsvergunning regulier voor onbepaalde tijd 6009 (PDF, 810.19 KB)

The residence permit

What else you need to know about your residence permit.

Working with the residence permit

It says on the back of your residence permit whether you are allowed to work. You are free to work in the Netherlands with a permanent residence permit. Your employer does not need to have a work permit (in Dutch: tewerkstellingsvergunning or TWV) for you. It says on your residence document ‘Free to work, work permit not required’ (in Dutch: Arbeid vrij toegestaan, TWV niet vereist).

Rights

With a residence permit for an 'EU long-term resident' or 'permanent residence permit' you have almost the same rights as someone with Dutch nationality. The difference is that you are not allowed to vote in national elections. And that you are not allowed to work in certain special government sectors. For example for the police or the army. In order to do so, you must first apply for Dutch nationality.

Legal obligations

You have an obligation to provide information. Find out more about your legal obligations on the page Obligations foreign national and sponsor of family member.

The IND checks whether you meet your obligations. What if you do not? Then you can get an administrative fine.

Other applications

Replace residence permit

Has your residence permit been damaged, lost or stolen? Then have the residence permit replaced. Visit the web page Residence permit damaged or change of personal details or Residence permit lost or stolen.

Renew permanent residence permit

A permanent residence permit is always issued for 5 years. When it expires, you can apply for renewal. Visit the web page Renew permanent residence permit

See also

Living in the Netherlands

Travelling with a residence permit and a return visa

What is a (ex-)privileged person?

Requirements

Process and costs

Application form

The residence permit

See also

title: Renew permanent residence permit
content:
Renew permanent residence permit

Last update: 23 September 2025

You want to renew the residence document of your residence permit for permanent residency. Find out what the requirements are and how to renew the residence document.

Requirements

Process and costs

Application forms

The residence document

See also

Requirements

Your residence document or card is about to expire. You wish to renew the residence document. The requirements are as follows:

You have legally resided in the Netherlands.

You have one of these documents:

Permanent regular residence permit (type II)

Long-term resident EC (type V)

Permanent asylum residence permit (type IV)

Permanent residence citizens of the European Union (type EU/EEA)

Permanent residency Withdrawal Agreement (Brexit)

Process and costs

These are the steps in the application process for renewal of your residence document.

Show details Hide details

1. Check requirements

A valid provisional residence permit (MVV) is needed for this application

Make sure you meet the requirements.

1. Check requirements

Make sure you meet the requirements.

2. Collect documents

The written application form lists which documents you need. Collect all documents before you apply. You can upload the documents with the online application.

3. Apply

You can submit this application online or in writing by post.

You can submit the application from 3 months before your current residence document expires.

4. Pay for application

The renewal of the residence document costs € 85,00. Are you under 18? Then a different amount applies. For all fees and exceptions, visit our web page Fees: costs of an application.

When you apply online, you pay the fee immediately with iDEAL internet banking. Are you submitting a written application? Then you will receive a letter explaining how you can pay.

5. Wait for decision on application

The IND must take a decision within 8 weeks. This is called the decision period.

We can extend the decision period if it takes longer to decide. For example, because the application is not complete. In that case, the IND will send a message.

Follow the application in My IND.

6. Decision on application

The IND first checks whether you meet the requirements of renewing your residence document.

The decision is in the form of a letter. The decision is positive or negative.

Positive decision: you will receive a new residence document.

Negative decision: you will not receive a residence document. You can object against this decision.

7. Collect residence permit

When the residence permit is ready at an IND desk, you will receive a letter. You must make an appointment to collect it. Make an appointment to collect the residence document.

Application forms

You can submit this application online. You must have DigiD with SMS verification and internet banking (iDEAL).

Online application form renewal

Apply online You need a DigiD

Written application forms

You can download the form if you prefer to fill in the application form and send it to us by post.

Application for replacement or renewal of foreign national’s document (6013)

Application replacement or renewal of a foreign nationals document 6013 (PDF, 270.98 KB)

The residence document

What else you need to know about the residence document.

Working with the residence document

With this residence permit, you are free to work in the Netherlands. Your employer does not need a work permit (in Dutch: tewerkstellingsvergunning or TWV) for you. It says on the back of your residence document ''Free to work, work permit not required'' (in Dutch: Arbeid vrij toegestaan, TWV niet vereist).

Rights

With a permanent residence permit, you have almost the same rights as someone with the Dutch nationality. The difference is that you are not allowed to vote in national elections. And that you are not allowed to work in certain special government sectors. For example, you cannot join the police force or the army.

See also

Becoming a Dutch Citizen by naturalisation

Requirements

Process and costs

Application forms

The residence document

See also

title: After your application
content:
After your application

After your application, you can make an appointment for biometrics or upload documents. You will also read about the status of your application.

elements.text: After your application
After your application, you can make an appointment for biometrics or upload documents. You will also read about the status of your application.

title: Biometrics appointment: photo, signature and fingerprints
content:
Biometrics appointment: photo, signature and fingerprints

Last update: 12 February 2026

You have received a letter from the IND. In the letter it says that we require your photo, signature and fingerprints for your residence document. On this page you make an appointment to provide your biometrics.

Have you fled Ukraine and received a letter stating that the IND needs your biometric data? Then make an appointment on the Biometrics Ukraine page. Ви виїхали з України і отримали лист про те, що IND необхідні ваші біометричні дані? Призначте зустріч на сторінці Біометрія України.

When to make an appointment

You can only make an appoint when you have received a letter from the IND. This can be in these situations:

You have submitted an application for a residence permit. And you have received a letter of receipt for this application from the IND.

You have received a letter from the IND that we need photo, signature and fingerprints to make your residence document.

When you make an appointment before receiving a letter, your appointment will be cancelled.

Make an appointment

At the Desk or Service Point, the IND will take a digital passport photo and take your fingerprints. You can choose which location you want to go to. Providing biometrics is free of charge.

Providing biometrics in Amsterdam, Den Haag, Haarlem, 's-Hertogenbosch, Zwolle, Enschede or Utrecht

Would you like to provide biometrics at an IND Desk in Amsterdam, Den Haag, Haarlem, 's-Hertogenbosch or Zwolle? Or at an Expat Centre in Enschede or Utrecht? Then you can make an appointment at the IND. There, the IND will take a digital passport photo and take your fingerprints.

Make appointment at IND

Providing biometrics in Maastricht

Do you live in the province of Limburg? Then you can make an appointment at the Expat Centre Maastricht. There, the IND will take a digital passport photo and take your fingerprints.

Make appointment in Maastricht

Providing biometrics in Goes

Do you live in the province of Zeeland or a municipality in the Brabantse Wal? Then you can make an appointment at the IND Service Point in Goes. There, the IND will take a digital passport photo and take your fingerprints.

Make appointment in Goes

Biometrics for a child under 1 year

Is the residence document for a child under 1 year old? Then do not make an appointment for providing biometrics. Please send a passport photo of the child to the using the Appendix Passport photo form. The passport photo must be taken by a photographer. Read more about the requirements for the passport photo.

Appropiate clothing for passport photo

We kindly ask you not to wear completely white or light grey clothing. The passport photo is taken against a light grey background. If you wear light clothing, the photo may turn out wrong.

You may keep your head covered for religious or philosophical reasons. The head covering must not cover your face. We also recommend wearing a plain colored head covering that is not light gray. If your head covering has reflective material or busy patterns, the photo may turn out wrong.

Information about your appointment

Show details Hide details

What to bring to your appointment

Bring the following documents to your appointment:

Your valid passport or other travel document.

Your appointment code. This code is in the email you receive when you make an appointment online.

Do or did you already have a residence permit in the Netherlands? Or a Foreign Nationals Identity Document (VI) type W or W2? Then bring your (expired) residence document or VI.

Have you lost your residence document or Foreign Nationals Identity Document (VI) type W or W2? Or has it been stolen? Then bring the report of the loss or theft reported to the police.

Appointment for a child age1 or older who was born in the Netherlands

As the parent of the child, you must bring the following documents to the appointment:

Your valid passport or valid residence document.

The child's valid passport, or the (copy of) birth certificate, or the (copy of) registration of the child in the Personal Records Database (in Dutch: Basisregistratie Personen or BRP) at the municipality.

Reschedule or cancel an appointment

Have you made an appointment, but are you unable to attend? It is not possible to reschedule your appointment. There is a link in the email with the appointment confirmation. Click on the link and cancel your appointment. After that, make a new appointment.

Have you made an appointment for multiple people? You can only cancel the entire appointment and not for just 1 person.

title: Decision periods
content:
Decision periods

Last update: 26 February 2026

The IND must decide on your residence permit application or your objection to an IND decision within a certain time frame. This is called the decision period. Find out the decision period for your residence permit application or objection.

Decision period per application

Start date of application decision period

Extension of decision period

Decision period for objection or appeal

Decision period per application

The decision period of an application is often determined by law. Does the statutory decision period end on a Saturday, Sunday or generally recognised holiday? Then the decision period will be extended until the next working day. If there is no legal decision period for an application, a reasonable decision period will apply. What is reasonable depends on the type of application.

Show details Hide details

Application for recognition as sponsor

A legal decision period of 3 months applies to this application.

Application for the purpose of residence of research

A legal decision period of 60 days applies to this application.

Was your application submitted by a recognised sponsor? Then the IND usually needs less time to decide. If the application is complete, the target decision period is 2 weeks.

Application European Blue Card

A decision period of 30 days applies to:

An application applied for by a recognised sponsor

An application for long-term mobility

An application for family members. The application must be submitted at the same time as an European Blue Card application, which is subject to a decision period of 30 days.

A decision period of 90 days applies to all other applications.

Application for the purpose of residence of study

A legal decision period of 60 days applies to this application.

Was your application submitted by a recognised sponsor? Then the IND usually needs less time to decide. If the application is complete, the target decision period is 2 weeks.

Application for the purpose of residence of work experience

A legal decision period of 60 days applies to this application.

Was your application submitted by a recognised sponsor? Then the IND usually needs less time to decide. If the application is complete, the target decision period is 2 weeks.

Application for the purpose of residence of exchange in the context of the European Solidarity Corps

A legal decision period of 60 days applies to this application.

Was your application submitted by a recognised sponsor? Then the IND usually needs less time to decide. If the application is complete, the target decision period is 2 weeks.

Application for the purpose of residence of seasonal work

A legal decision period of 90 days applies to this application.

Did you previously have a residence ​permit for seasonal work in the Netherlands? And was this no longer than 5 years ago? Then the IND will take a decision on your application within 60 days.​​​

Other applications regular temporary residence permit

A legal decision period of 90 days applies to the following applications:

Regular temporary residence permit without a provisional residence permit (in Dutch: machtiging tot voorlopig verblijf or MVV)

Regular temporary residence permit with MVV; this is the procedure for entry and residence (in Dutch Toegang en Verblijf or TEV)

Extension of regular temporary residence permit

Change of residence purpose of regular temporary residence permit

Application permanent residence permit or for a long-term EU resident

A legal decision period of 6 months applies to this application.

Application temporary or permanent asylum residence permit

A legal decision period of 6 months applies to these applications.

A temporary asylum residence permit before 1 January 2024

Did you apply for temporary asylum before 1 January 2024? Then the legal decision period for your application is 15 months. From 27 September 2022, the legal decision period of 6 months has been temporary extended by 9 months. This applied to asylum applications for which the decision period of 6 months had not yet expired on 27 September 2022. And it applies to asylum applications submitted between 27 September 2022 and 1 January 2024.

Decision sometimes later

Unfortunately, it may also take longer before you receive a decision. Many asylum applications are still awaiting a decision and many asylum applications are still being made.

Application for family reunification by a family member with an asylum residence permit

A legal decision period of 9 months applies to this application.

Application permanent residence for EU/EEA and Swiss nationals

A reasonable decision period of 90 days applies to this application.

Application verification against EU law (proof of lawful residence)

A legal decision period of 6 months applies to this application.

Application residence with your minor Dutch child (Chavez)

A legal decision period of 6 months applies to this application.

Application long-term residence for family members of EU/EEA and Swiss nationals

A legal decision period of 90 days applies to this application.

Application Withdrawal Agreement (permanent) residence document

Withdrawal Agreement permanent residence document

A legal decision period of 90 days applies to this application.

Withdrawal Agreement residence document

A legal decision period of 6 months applies to this application.

Application replacement or extension of residence document

A reasonable decision period of 8 weeks applies to the following applications:

Replacement of residence document in case of theft, loss, damage or change of personal details

Extension of permanent residence document, long-term EU resident or permanent residence

Application Foreign Nationals Identity Document Type W and Type W2

A reasonable decision period of 8 weeks applies to this application.

Application for naturalisation

A legal decision period of 1 year applies to this application.

Start date of application decision period

The decision period starts on the day the IND receives the application.

Show details Hide details

Start date of asylum application decision period

The decision period of a first asylum application starts on the day you inform a government organization in the Netherlands that you want asylum.

Have you applied for a permanent asylum residence permit? Then the decision period will start no earlier than 4 weeks before the current permit expires.

Start date of naturalisation application decision period

The decision period of an application for naturalisation starts when the application is complete. And when you have paid the costs of the application.

Extension of decision period

The IND can extend the decision period if more time is needed. Then the IND will notify you. Depending on the application, the IND determines by how long the decision period will be extended.

Show details Hide details

Extension of short-stay visa decision period

The IND can extend the decision period by a maximum of 45 days if extra information or investigation is needed.

Extension of application for recognition as sponsor decision period

The IND can extend the decision period by 6 months if extra information or investigation is needed.

Extension of regular application decision period

The decision period varies per application. The IND can extend the decision period by several weeks to a maximum of 6 months. If your application is not complete, you or your authorised representative will receive a letter. You can send the documents within 2 weeks. Then the decision period will be extended by 2 weeks.

Extension of asylum application decision period

From 27 September 2022 until 1 January 2024, the IND has extended the legal decision period by 9 months. This means that the legal decision period is 15 months for these asylum applications.

There was also an extension of the decision period for applications submitted from 1 January 2024. The IND has reversed this extension.

In the following situations, the IND can also extend the decision period:

In exceptional situations, the IND can extend the decision period by another 3 months.

In the event of a postponement of decision, the IND can extend the decision period by a maximum of 1 year. The Minister for Asylum and Migration has decided that for the time being no decision will be taken on the application. This happens if the Minister for Asylum and Migration expects that a country will be unsafe for a short time.

The IND can extend the legal decision period to a maximum of 21 months.

Extension of naturalisation application decision period

The IND can extend the decision period by 6 months. After this, the decision period can be extended once more by another 6 months. The IND can extend the decision period by 1 year in total.

Decision period for objection or appeal

The decision period starts on the first day after the final date on which you can send the letter of objection by post. This is the case even if you object to the decision earlier.

Show details Hide details

Decision period for objection to short-term visa application decision

The IND will decide on the objection to the decision within 6 weeks. The decision period can be extended for another 6 weeks. Therefore, the IND must have decided on your objection within a total of 12 weeks.

Decision period for objection to regular application decision

The IND decides on the objection to the decision within 6 to 19 weeks. The decision period depends on the type of application. The IND can extend the decision period by 6 weeks.

Is the objection letter not complete?

Then you or your authorised representative will receive a letter from the IND saying that you must complete the objection. The IND letter also says that it will extend the decision period by 2 weeks. If investigation is necessary, the IND can extend the decision period by a maximum of 13 weeks.

Decision period for objection to naturalisation application

The IND decides on the objection within 6 weeks. The IND can extend the decision period by 6 weeks.

Decision period after appeal

You can appeal to a court against the rejection of your asylum application or objection. What happens if the court declares your appeal well-founded and the IND has to take a new decision? Then the decision period will be the same as for the first decision. Except if the judge sets a different period in its decision. A well-founded appeal means that the court proves you are completely or partly right.

The IND decides on the objection within 6 weeks. The IND can extend the decision period by 6 weeks.
Decision period after appeal

You can appeal to a court against the rejection of your asylum application or objection. What happens if the court declares your appeal well-founded and the IND has to take a new decision? Then the decision period will be the same as for the first decision. Except if the judge sets a different period in its decision. A well-founded appeal means that the court proves you are completely or partly right.

title: Uploading documents
content:
Uploading documents

Last update: 24 February 2026

You often have to upload documents with information for your application. Find out when and how you can upload documents.

Uploading documents after your application

Uploading documents during your online application

Digitising documents for upload

Uploading documents after your application

Upload documents

You have submitted an application, objected against a decision or started another procedure. The IND is now processing the application. You can upload additional documents in the following situations:

Your application, objection or other procedures is still in process.

In a letter, the IND has asked for supplementary information.

You would like to add documents to your application yourself.

Are you a business customer and have u submitted an application or reported a change in the situation? Read more about uploading documents in the Business Portal.

Show details Hide details

Rules for uploading

Take the following rules into account when uploading documents.

Your application or other procedure still must still be in progress with the IND. Have you appealed decision to court? Then you no longer can upload documents to the IND.

You must be one of these persons:

The applicant;

Your partner’s or family member’s sponsor;

The specially authorised person who objects on behalf of an applicant for a short-stay Schengen visa (to visit family or friends). You have received permission from the applicant for the objection.

You must have a DigiD account with an SMS code to log in. Do you have no DigiD? Then send the documents to the IND by post.

You must fill in the case number. The case number is written in the acknowledgement of receipt by the IND. This is the letter which says that the IND has received your application.

Only upload PDFs without password protection.

Sending documents by post

Are you unable to upload documents or do you not want to do this? Or is your application or other process no longer being processed? Then send the documents to the IND by post. The postal address and the rules for sending documents are on our Contact page.

Documents via authorised person or lawyer

Have you been authorised or are you an applicant’s lawyer? Then you are personally responsible for processing personal data. It is important that you check all documents carefully for any possible data leaks before sending them. This way, you ensure that other people’s personal data do not end up in the IND file by accident. So, please take the following into consideration:

Each time, only send applications or documents of the applicant. Except if you think parts of other people’s personal data are necessary.

Personal data of applicant must only be visible to that person. Not to others. Only if the applicant has a parent or legal representative does this not apply.

Our privacy statement explains how the IND shares personal data.

The IND adds the documents it receives to the applicant’s file. The Public Records Act (in Dutch: Archiefwet) applies to the file.

Uploading documents during your online application

Do you want to start the online application? Then first make sure all required documents are available digitally. Perhaps you must still digitise some documents first. You can only upload documents that have been digitised correctly.

Show details Hide details

Uploading file

Choose ‘Selecteer een bestand’ (select a file) in the application.

Go to the place on your device where the passport has been stored in PDF. Only upload PDFs without password protection.

Select the file. Then choose the button ‘Openen’ (open).

On the screen of the online application, it now says that the document has been uploaded.

Files of up to 8 MB

Make sure that your files are less than 8 MB. Together, all documents must be no more than 40 MB. If your file is larger than 8 MB you will see this message on the screen.

‘Waarschuwing te groot document in online aanvraag’ (Warning, online application contains file that is too large)

Image description: Screenshot of a part of the online application. At the top it says ‘Paspoort’ (passport). Under it, there are two horizontal bars, the top one red and the bottom one pink. The red bar says in small white letters ‘Maximale bestandsgrootte is 8 MB’ (Maximum file size is 8 MB). At the right of the pink bar it says in white letters ‘Selecteer een bestand’ (select a file).

Several PDF files in one upload

Sometimes you can add several PDFs to a requested upload. For example when you upload your income details. Then choose ‘Selecteer een bestand’ (select a file) for each file. This is the only way to add each of the PDFs. In our example we have added 3 files to the online application.

Upload of 3 documents in an online application

Image description: Screenshot of a part of the online application. At the top, it says 'Bewijsstukken Arbeid in Loondienst' (supporting document paid employment). Under it, there are two horizontal bars, the top one blue and the bottom one white. In the blue bar it says ‘Selecteer een bestand’ (select a file) in white letters. The white bar contains two buttons, from left to right: ‘Bestand’ (file) and ‘Grootte’ (size), with a green line under them. Under the button 'Bestand' (file), these three PDF documents are listed: Test1.pdf, Test 2.pdf and Test3.pdf. Under the heading ' Grootte ' it says that the size of each document is 3 MB. For each document, there is a hyperlink reading 'Verwijder' (delete) behind 3 MB.

Deleting file after uploading

Click 'Verwijder' (delete) behind the file you would like to remove. The document will then disappear from the list of uploaded documents.

Deletion of uploaded document in online application

Image description: Screenshot of a part of the online application. At the top, it says 'Bewijsstukken Arbeid in Loondienst' (supporting document paid employment). Under it, there are two horizontal bars, the top one blue and the bottom one white. In the blue bar it says ‘Selecteer een bestand’ (select a file) in white letters. The white bar contains two buttons, from left to right: ‘Bestand’ (file) and ‘Grootte’ (size), with a green line under them. Under the button 'Bestand' (file), these two PDF documents are listed: Test 2.pdf and Test3.pdf. Under the heading ' Grootte ' it says that the size of each document is 3 MB. For each document, there is a hyperlink reading 'Verwijder' (delete) behind 3 MB.

Digitising documents for upload

Digitising is converting documents to a digital form for use on a computer or mobile device. Are you going to digitise something? Then pay attention to these important rules.

The IND only accepts files of the types PDF (without password protection) and JPG.

For some applications, you can also upload a JPEG file.

Give digitised documents a clear name.

You cannot upload a PDF portfolio with combined PDF files.

Find out how to digitise documents and what you need to pay attention to.

Show details Hide details

Digitising on mobile device

Do you have a smartphone, tablet or other mobile device? Then you can use a range of scanner apps from your app store, for example Adobe Scan. Choose one that saves the documents as PDFs. Do not password protect PDFs for the IND.

Scanner apps use the camera in your phone or tablet to digitise the document. First, you must put the document on a dark background. Then you open the app and the camera will digitise your document. Save the document as a PDF or JPG file.

Digitising with scanner

You can also scan documents and save them on your computer. For example using a printer that is also suitable for scanning. What if your scanner does not save documents as PDF or JPG right away? Then you must still do this yourself.

Digitising at copy shop or print shop

What if you do not have a mobile device or scanner? Then you can have your documents digitised at a copy or print shop.

Converting to PDF or JPG

There is a lot of information on the Internet for converting digital documents to PDF or JPG.

Combining documents

There is a lot of information on the Internet about combining PDF and JPG files. The IND is unable to open a PDF portfolio.
